# Single-factor linear model & the design matrix

Many studies compare **groups**: control vs treatment, inshore vs offshore. Here we use an **ocean-acidification (OA)** experiment — fish reared in control vs acidified seawater — and ask whether acidification changes their response to predators. 

In this experiment fish were given a choice to swim towards or away from a predator cue. The response variable is the time spent near the predator cue, and the predictor is the treatment (control vs acidified). 

## Coding a two level factor in FANGS

FANGS auto-encodes a text column like `treatment` (`"normal seawater"`, `"acidified seawater"`) into integers for you. The first group becomes a `0`, the second group becomes a `1`. You can then write the model directly against the indicator, e.g. `mu[i] <- alpha + beta * treatment[i]`. 

Because treatment is `0` for the control group, `alpha` is the control mean. Because treatment is `1` for the acidified group, `alpha + beta` is the acidified mean. So $\beta$ is the difference between acidified and control fish — the **treatment effect**.

UP TO HERE. CHeck that -1 is correct (will work if indexing starts at 1, but not if it starts at 0).
Also why does below model sample more poorll than the next model

```
model {
  for (i in 1:N) {
    time[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i], sigma)
    mu[i] <- alpha + beta_trt * (treatment[i]-1)
  }
  alpha ~ dnorm(50, 50)
   beta_trt ~ dnorm(0, 10)
  sigma ~ dunif(0, 50)
}
```

```
model {
  for (i in 1:N) {
    time[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i], sigma)
    mu[i] <- beta_trt[treatment[i]]
  }
  for (j in 1:2) {
    beta_trt[j] ~ dnorm(0, 10)
  }
  sigma ~ dunif(0, 50)
}
```


## Coding a 2-level factor as a design matrix 


A categorical predictor enters the model through a **design matrix**. With two levels we use a single **0/1 indicator**:

| Treatment | indicator `trt` |
|-----------|-----------------|
| Control   | 0 |
| Acidified | 1 |

$$
y_i = \alpha + \beta\,\text{trt}_i
$$

- $\alpha$ — the **control** mean (when `trt = 0`).
- $\beta$ — the **difference** for acidified fish (the treatment effect).

If $\beta$'s 95% CI excludes 0, acidification credibly shifts the response.


## Your task

Fit the single-factor model to `oa-study.csv` and check your posterior means for
the control mean $\alpha$ and the acidification effect.
